symptoms.symptom_name: Sensation of skipped beat followed by a strong heavy 'thud', worse lying quietly at night
symptoms.character: skipped beat then sudden forceful thud, with heightened awareness of the heartbeat
symptoms.onset: onset this evening on lying down
symptoms.duration: intermittent, on and off through the evening

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Benign ventricular ectopic beats (premature ventricular contractions)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The skipped-beat-then-forceful-thud pattern, worse at rest and with heightened attention, is the classic subjective description of premature ventricular contractions with a compensatory pause and augmented post-ectopic beat.
SVT typically presents as sustained rapid regular palpitations rather than an intermittent skipped/extra-beat pattern over a regular baseline.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy coffee consumption today, much more than usual

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Benign ventricular ectopic beats (premature ventricular contractions)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excess caffeine is a well-established precipitant of ventricular ectopy via increased sympathetic drive and catecholamine sensitivity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of true syncope, exertional chest pain, or resting dyspnoea (brief lightheadedness only)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Benign ventricular ectopic beats (premature ventricular contractions)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of exertional or haemodynamically concerning symptoms supports a benign, non-life-threatening cause of the ectopy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ischaemic heart disease / acute coronary syndrome
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete absence of exertional/resting chest pain, dyspnoea, or diaphoresis argues strongly against an ischaemic cause of the symptom.
Structural or inherited arrhythmic conditions more typically present with exertional symptoms or true syncope; their absence lowers (without fully excluding) this concern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long working hours, heavy fatigue, and sleep deprivation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Benign ventricular ectopic beats (premature ventricular contractions)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue and sleep deprivation increase sympathetic tone and are recognised precipitants of symptomatic ectopy and heightened symptom awareness.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Panic attack / health anxiety with somatic amplification
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exhaustion and sleep deprivation also predispose to an amplified anxiety response, increasing the subjective severity of the symptom experience.

symptoms.symptom_name: No family history of sudden cardiac death or inherited heart conditions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a significant family history reduces concern for an inherited arrhythmic or structural cardiac condition underlying the ventricular ectopy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular underlying rhythm interrupted by a premature beat with a compensatory pause; premature loud heart sounds and forceful post-ectopic beat on auscultation; no murmurs/gallops, no elevated JVP; lungs clear (clinician-obtained findings)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Benign ventricular ectopic beats (premature ventricular contractions)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A regular rhythm interrupted by premature beats with a compensatory pause and forceful post-ectopic beat, on a structurally normal examination, strongly supports benign ventricular ectopy — but requires clinician auscultation/palpation, not obtainable via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG showing broad, unassociated (non-P-preceded) QRS complexes consistent with ventricular ectopy, otherwise normal (formal documentation pending via GP)

investigation_details.expected_result: Broad, unassociated QRS complexes consistent with ventricular ectopics anticipated on formal tracing, with an otherwise normal ECG; formal documentation via a proper tracing is still pending through the GP — should be recommended, not assumed already complete.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without a formally documented ECG, conduction abnormalities or QT prolongation that could predispose to a more serious arrhythmia cannot be excluded, and the ectopic morphology is not confirmed, even though the clinical picture is otherwise reassuring.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Benign ventricular ectopic beats (premature ventricular contractions)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Broad, unassociated QRS complexes on ECG confirm ventricular ectopy and provide baseline rhythm documentation.

A normal baseline ECG (QT interval, conduction) substantially reduces concern for an underlying conduction abnormality or inherited arrhythmia syndrome, though a single ECG does not entirely exclude paroxysmal disorders.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Benign ventricular ectopic beats (premature ventricular contractions)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of intermittent (not sustained) skipped-beat-then-thud palpitations, identifiable reversible triggers (heavy caffeine, fatigue, poor sleep), absence of syncope/exertional/haemodynamic red flags, and no concerning family history together support a benign explanation not requiring emergency escalation, while still warranting routine ECG documentation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Recommended for baseline rhythm documentation, to confirm the ectopic morphology, and to exclude conduction abnormalities/QT prolongation — appropriately routine (not urgent) given the reassuring history and absence of red flags.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes (including potassium) and thyroid function tests
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reasonable baseline screen for reversible contributors to ectopy (hypokalaemia, thyroid dysfunction), consistent with the source note's own care plan.

Not first-line for a single self-resolving episode with a reassuring history and identifiable triggers; indicated only if symptoms persist/recur frequently or the baseline ECG is abnormal — recommending it immediately over-investigates this presentation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department troponin / urgent cardiology referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated given the absence of chest pain, exertional symptoms, true syncope, or concerning family history; recommending this would represent inappropriate over-triage.
